title: Water Resource Quality Degradation Pathways from Improper E-Waste Management
description: A flowchart illustrating how improper disposal and recycling of electronic waste can lead to the contamination of surface and groundwater resources, impacting both availability and quality.
relationships: Landfill leachate and runoff from recycling sites percolates into the soil
Toxic contaminants seep into and pollute the groundwater aquifer
Polluted runoff flows into rivers, lakes, and other surface water bodies
Contaminated sources enter the water treatment system, which may not remove all e-waste toxins
Treated water is distributed to communities, potentially carrying residual contaminants
